div.p: Process address space showing allocation zones and data lifetimes

ul.li: char* value from strdup() in lexer
Size: 32 bytes per node + union data
Lifetime: Same as containing AST node

div.p: Address Content Owner ──────────────────────────────────────────── 0x555555600000 [Input line buffer] getline 0x555555601000 [Token: "echo"] lexer 0x555555601010 [Token: "hello"] lexer 0x555555601020 [ASTNode: SIMPLE] parser 0x555555601040 [argv[0]: "echo"] AST 0x555555601050 [argv[1]: "hello"] AST 0x555555601060 [Redirect: NULL] AST ... 0x555555700000 [Heap break]

ol.li: Input line: Allocated by getline(), freed by main loop at end of iteration
Token strings: Allocated by lexer via strdup(), freed by parser after consuming or on error
AST nodes: Allocated by create_node(), freed by free_ast() after execution
argv arrays: Allocated during parsing, freed recursively by free_ast()
Expansion results: Allocated by expand_word(), freed by expand_result_free()

ul.li: REPL iteration: Input line → tokens → AST → execute → free all
No leaks: Every malloc has matching free
No use-after-free: Clear pointers after freeing in structs
Child processes: Use _exit() not exit() to avoid double-free of stdio buffers
